title: EDI Acknowledgement Monitoring
spl:
index=edi sourcetype="edi:control"
| stats earliest(_time) as sent latest(ack_time) as ack by interchange_id, doc_type, partner_id
| eval ack_latency_sec=ack-sent
| where isnull(ack) OR ack_latency_sec>3600
| sort -sent
description: Missing 997/999/CONTRL acks for ASNs or orders disrupt supply-chain and inventory ‚Äî common in automotive / discrete manufacturing.
value: Missing 997/999/CONTRL acks for ASNs or orders disrupt supply-chain and inventory ‚Äî common in automotive / discrete manufacturing.
implementation: SLA per trading partner; auto-retry and partner escalation on NAK codes.
visualization: Table (late/missing acks), Line chart (ack latency trend), Bar chart (by partner).
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance‚Äîtriage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: B2B gateway (IBM/Seeburger), VAN logs.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="edi:control"`, `sourcetype="as2:mdn"`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
SLA per trading partner; auto-retry and partner escalation on NAK codes.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edi sourcetype="edi:control"
| stats earliest(_time) as sent latest(ack_time) as ack by interchange_id, doc_type, partner_id
| eval ack_latency_sec=ack-sent
| where isnull(ack) OR ack_latency_sec>3600
| sort -sent
```

Understanding this SPL

**EDI Acknowledgement Monitoring** ‚Äî Missing 997/999/CONTRL acks for ASNs or orders disrupt supply-chain and inventory ‚Äî common in automotive / discrete manufacturing.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="edi:control"`, `sourcetype="as2:mdn"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): B2B gateway (IBM/Seeburger), VAN logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edi; **sourcetype**: edi:control. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=edi, sourcetype="edi:control". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by interchange_id, doc_type, partner_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_latency_sec** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(ack) OR ack_latency_sec>3600` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (late/missing acks), Line chart (ack latency trend), Bar chart (by partner).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: edi acknowledgement before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
